scenario: Meridian Payments runs Camunda 8.8 with 22 job workers processing 14000 payment authorizations per hour. One job type, settle-batch, must raise max-jobs-active to 64 while every other worker keeps the defaults baseline. The team is structuring its application.yaml under camunda.client.worker.override.
question: Which key does the 8.8 reference document under camunda.client.worker.override for selecting a specific worker?

options.text: The 8.8 reference documents the key of camunda.client.worker.override as the job type the override applies to under each entry in the table.
The 8.8 reference documents the key of camunda.client.worker.override as the Spring bean name the override applies to under each entry in the table.
The 8.8 reference documents the key of camunda.client.worker.override as the BPMN process ID the override applies to under each entry in the table.
The 8.8 reference documents the key of camunda.client.worker.override as the worker owner name the override applies to under each entry in the table.

a.text: Correct. The camunda.client.worker.override section explicitly says the key of the override is the job type, with each row keyed by <job-type>.

b.text: Incorrect. The override key is the job type, not the Spring bean name; bean name is part of the auto-generated owner identifier.

c.text: Incorrect. Overrides are keyed by job type, not by the BPMN process ID; the BPMN process is unaware of worker configuration.

d.text: Incorrect. The owner name is set via the name property under the override entry; the override entry itself is keyed by job type.

explanation: The camunda.client.worker.override block in the 8.8 properties reference is a map whose key is the job type. Each entry overrides settings for the worker that handles that job type.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot starter properties reference
